key_insight: Mathematical reasoning can be viewed as settling into deep attractor basins in an LLM’s latent energy landscape, with correct solutions emerging as robust minima and hallucinations as unstable sharp minima.
engineering_relevance: A Gibbs-weighted multi-path inference scheme offers a physics-inspired way to improve reasoning reliability by approximating equilibrium retrieval rather than relying on greedy decoding.
method: Sample multiple reasoning trajectories from the model
Compute a spectral-entropy-based energy score for each path
Weight paths by Gibbs inverse-energy probability (P ∝ e^{-βE}) and select the most stable solution
evidence_signal: On GSM8K, the approach improved Microsoft Phi-3.5 accuracy from 84.7% to 90.1%, a gain of 5.38% according to the abstract.
watch_item: The method adds inference-time sampling and energy estimation overhead, so latency, calibration of β, and stability of the scoring proxy may limit deployment efficiency.
paper_title: Reasoning as Attractor Dynamics: Latent Memory Retrieval via Gibbs-Weighted Energy Minimization
